articles.title: Providence’s Duncan Powell pleads not guilty to charges resulting from altercation with woman
articles.description:
The woman told police that Providence basketball player Duncan Powell made multiple calls to her and threatened to use a gun to kill everyone in the house and then himself.

The post Providence’s Duncan Powell pleads not guilty to charges resulting from altercation with woman appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Duncan Powell, a Providence basketball player, pleads not guilty to threatening a woman.
The woman reported multiple threatening calls from Powell, including gun violence threats.
This incident raises concerns about safety and violence in the local community.

articles.title: Vermont labor board orders state to end return-to-office requirement for employees
articles.description:
The state has already filed a notice of appeal with the Vermont Supreme Court, officials said.

The post Vermont labor board orders state to end return-to-office requirement for employees appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Vermont labor board mandates end of return-to-office policy for state employees.
State officials have filed an appeal with the Vermont Supreme Court.
Boston residents may see similar labor disputes affecting remote work policies.

articles.title: Jordan’s Furniture will refund customers if both UConn teams make the NCAA title games
articles.description:
Some 20,000 Jordan’s Furniture customers could be fully repaid for $50 million worth of sales if the two teams win their Final Four games on Friday and Saturday.

The post Jordan’s Furniture will refund customers if both UConn teams make the NCAA title games appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jordan's Furniture offers refunds if both UConn teams reach NCAA title games.
Approximately 20,000 customers could receive refunds totaling $50 million.
This promotion directly impacts Boston residents who purchased furniture during the sales.

articles.title: 3 state troopers plead not guilty to charges connected to death of recruit after boxing match
articles.description:
Three members of the State Police pleaded not guilty Thursday in connection with the death of a recruit who suffered a concussion during a sparring session and blunt force injuries a day later.

The post 3 state troopers plead not guilty to charges connected to death of recruit after boxing match appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Three state troopers pleaded not guilty on April 2 regarding a recruit's death.
The recruit suffered a concussion during a sparring session before dying from injuries.
This case raises concerns about safety protocols in Boston's State Police training.

articles.title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
articles.description:
"I have had 13 incredible years in this league and I cannot wait to see what this next chapter holds."

The post Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Stephon Gilmore, former Patriots cornerback, retires after 13 NFL seasons.
Gilmore played a key role in the Patriots' 2019 Super Bowl win.
His retirement marks the end of an era for Boston sports fans.

articles.title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
articles.description:
The company has offices in Burlington, Mass. and Nashua, New Hampshire, but no layoffs have yet appeared in state filings.

The post Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Oracle has initiated global layoffs, affecting its operations in Burlington, Mass.
No layoffs have been reported in Massachusetts state filings as of now.
Local workforce uncertainty remains as Oracle's future plans in the region are unclear.
